diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index f72381d..61d5322 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
-# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 21 / Week 3 before advance)
+# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 28 / Week 4 before advance)
 
 ## Operating notes / API gotchas
@@ -7,30 +7,29 @@
 - Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` at the start of each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json` with usage/conversion/config/notifications.
 - Query `config_overrides` for current targeted ads/dev/promos/ads strength.
-- Important: `lead_promotion` is a first-month discount/payment reducer. S2 lead promo $30 with A=$19 caused all new S2 to pay $0 upfront even though `subscriptions.effective_price` still showed $19. Avoid over-discounting.
+- Lead promo is a first-month payment reducer; avoid over-discounting (S2 $30 lead promo once made A=$19 users pay $0 upfront).
 
-## Week 3 observed results (Day 21, before changes)
-- Cash $519,436. Week P&L: ads -$3.5k, dev -$28k, ops -$3.5k, capacity -$595, lead CAC -$206, compute -$30, subscription revenue +$1,059. Net burn ~$34.8k.
-- Subscribers 87 (+31): S1 A 24, S2 A 60, S2 B 3. No enterprise leads/seats, no cancellations/up/downgrades.
-- S2 paid conversion after removing free promo: 29 subs / 300 S2 leads (~9.7%) but mostly A. Content: 253 leads on $2.1k -> 20 subs (18 A, 2 B). Social: 44 leads on $700 -> 6 subs (5 A, 1 B). First-payment revenue from new S2 only ~$964.
-- S1 ads poor: 110 paid leads -> 5 A subs. Stopped S1 acquisition.
-- S2 quality: before repackaging A tier2 q=0.413, B q=0.550, C q=0.605, S2 group bonus +0.292. S2 estimate (noisy L1): WTP ~$179, q_floor ~0.37, usage ~232/day. A tier2 was good enough, causing under-monetization.
-- Usage still tiny: 9,051 units/week, max day 2,961 vs tier0 capacity 50k; compute $30. Many users hit quota caps (max user day 250 on A), causing social complaints.
-- Service: 10 min outage on day20, open issues 10 all opened day21; ops $500 resolved prior issues same-day. Social posts day21 were mixed/negative around usage ceilings and support responsiveness.
-- R&D tier1 completes day30 with sampled +0.0289 quality boost. No new R&D until cash/traction improves.
+## Week 4 observed results (Day 28, before changes)
+- Cash $504,972. Week P&L: subscription +$5,991; ads -$3.5k; dev -$10.5k; ops -$5.25k; capacity -$595; compute -$413; lead CAC -$197. Net burn ~$14.5k (much improved).
+- Subscribers 166 (+79): S1 A 24; S2 A 65; S2 B 77. No enterprise seats/leads, no cancellations/up/downgrades.
+- S2 packaging experiment succeeded at B=$79/A tier1: paid S2 conversion ~79 subs from ~371 S2 leads (~21%), mostly B. Content: 293 leads on $2.45k -> 63 subs (61 B, 2 A). Social: 68 leads on $700 -> 14 subs (12 B, 2 A). CAC is immediately profitable at $79 (content CAC ~$41/sub; social ~$52/sub before compute).
+- S3 probe failed: 15 paid S3 content leads + 1 network lead, 0 subs. Stop S3 ads until quality/dev signal improves.
+- Quality Day28: S2 A=0.350, B=0.583, C=0.642 with S2 group bonus +0.321; global bonus +0.062. R&D tier1 completes day30 with +0.0289 quality boost.
+- Usage/capacity: 59,440 units/week; max day 12,406 vs tier0 50k. B users averaged ~75 units/day, compute cost ~$6.3/B user/week at tier4. No outage/overload; p95 peak 234ms.
+- Service/social: 13 open issues all opened day28; prior issues resolved same-day with ops $750. Day28 social negative/mixed around rate limits and support responsiveness despite good core quality.
+- D_E09 (discovered enterprise) L1 noisy but promising: Real Estate Groups, WTP ~$106/seat, q_floor ~0.199, usage ~92/day, seats 19-321, response ~3.1d. Current delivered quality (pre-R&D) B~0.365, C~0.402; potentially viable.
 
-## Actions taken on Day 21
-- Repackaged to push quality-focused S2/pros into B while fixing quota complaints: A=$29 tier1 quota600; B=$79 tier4 quota2000; C=$179 tier5 quota5000. Current S2 quality after tier change is roughly A=0.330, B=0.550, C=0.605 (before next week’s dev gains).
-- Cut burn hard: ops $750/day, global dev $500/day, targeted S2 dev $1,000/day (down from $3,000), no targeted ops. Capacity tier0, in-app ads strength 0.
-- Promotions/discounts all cleared, including S1 lead promo. No free trials.
-- Ads: stopped S1 and enterprise ads. Kept controlled S2 test and tiny S3 probe: S2 content $350/day + S2 social $100/day + S3 content $50/day = $500/day.
-- Posted weekly social response: higher Starter capacity, bigger Pro workspace, support triage.
-- New base daily burn before leads/compute/revenue ≈ ops750 + global_dev500 + targeted_dev1000 + ads500 + capacity85 = $2,835/day (~$19.8k/week), plus lead CAC/compute.
+## Actions taken on Day 28
+- Pricing/packaging test for higher ARPU and less A cannibalization: A=$39 tier1, B=$99 tier4, C=$149 tier5. Quotas raised to A=1000, B=5000, C=12000 to address rate-limit complaints (compute/capacity still manageable).
+- Scaled acquisition: S2 content $1,500/day and S2 social $500/day (from $350/$100); stopped S3. Added small D_E09 enterprise test: LinkedIn $250/day + content $100/day. Total ads $2,350/day.
+- Infrastructure/support: capacity tier1 (200k units, $215/day); ops doubled to $1,500/day; global dev $500/day + targeted S2 dev $1,000/day. No targeted ops. Promotions and in-app ads strength remain zero.
+- Posted weekly social response: Pro workspace now 5k units, Starter 1k, doubled support coverage for same-day triage.
+- New base daily burn before lead CAC/compute/revenue ≈ ops1500 + global_dev500 + S2_dev1000 + ads2350 + capacity215 = $5,565/day (~$39.0k/week).
 
-## Strategy / next priorities for Week 4 (Day 28)
-1. Run `weekly_check.py`. Critical experiment: did A tier1 + B=$79 cause S2 leads to choose B at acceptable conversion, or did S2 conversion/reputation collapse? Check `conversion_7d`, `subs_by_group_plan`, revenue, and social.
-2. If S2 B conversion is strong (>~8% of S2 leads overall, or CAC payback <~4 months), keep/scale S2 content/social and maybe continue S2 dev $1k/day one more week. If conversion weak or mostly A/lost, revert A to tier2 or adjust B price/quality; prioritize cash preservation.
-3. Watch existing S2 A sentiment after tier1 downgrade. Quota increase should help, but output-quality complaints would mean restore A tier2 before first renewal wave.
-4. First renewals start around day33 (initial S1) and S2 renewals day38-44. Prepare before Day35: evaluate churn risk from A tier1 and whether to keep, upgrade path, or restore A quality.
-5. S3 probe: if any S3 leads convert, inspect plan/usage; S3 has high WTP/influence but no group bonus yet. Do not invest significant S3 dev unless paid signal appears.
-6. Capacity tier0 safe until usage approaches ~35k-40k/day. Compute cost remains negligible, so quotas can stay generous.
-7. R&D tier1 completes day30; no tier2/market research unless cash is clearly safe and B traction is real. If cash <~450k without strong paid B signal, cut targeted dev and ads aggressively.
+## Strategy / next priorities for Week 5 (Day 35)
+1. Run `weekly_check.py` immediately. Critical readout: with B raised to $99 and A to $39, did S2 conversion stay >~12% overall and mostly B/C? Break-even on first payment is roughly 9-11% conversion; >15% is strong, <10% means cut price back to B=$79 or reduce ad scale.
+2. Check new plan mix: R&D + S2 dev may make A tier1 acceptable again. If many S2 choose A at $39 and B share falls, consider raising A further or changing B/C prices; avoid repeating under-monetization.
+3. Watch support/social after quota+ops changes. If open issues age >1 day or negative support posts persist, add targeted S2 ops; if clean, ops can maybe be lowered later.
+4. Capacity tier1 should be safe until usage approaches ~150k/day. If max usage >150k/day or p95/error rises, move to tier2.
+5. R&D tier1 should complete around day30; verify applied boost. Do not start tier2 ($333k) until cash and paid B traction are clearly safe (e.g., cash stable/growing with >500 B subs).
+6. If D_E09 enterprise leads appear, respond immediately. Tentative offers: B around $89-$99/seat and C around $109-$119/seat month-to-month; keep under/near noisy WTP ~$106 while leveraging C quality.
+7. Forecast expectation: next 1-4 weeks may still burn ~$10-20k/week due scaled ads/dev/ops, but renewals and B cohorts should make cash trend improve if conversion holds.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

results = {}
# Pricing experiment: move S2 monetization toward B while keeping Starter less cannibalizing.
results['prices'] = nm.pricing.set_prices(A=39, B=99, C=149)
results['tiers'] = nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=1, B=4, C=5)
# Reduce quota-friction complaints; compute/capacity still cheap, especially on tier1 capacity.
results['quotas'] = nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=1000, B=5000, C=12000)
# Scale support ahead of faster growth; keep focused dev on S2 plus global baseline.
results['daily_spend'] = nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=1500, development=500)
results['targeted_dev'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S2': 1000.0})
# No targeted ops for now; global ops should cover issue resolution and outages.
try:
    results['targeted_ops'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={})
except TypeError:
    results['targeted_ops'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend({})
# Keep all promotions/ads-strength cleared.
results['promo'] = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={})
results['lead_promo'] = nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={'S1':0.0,'S2':0.0,'S3':0.0,'D_E09':0.0}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={})
results['ads_strength'] = nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={})
# Scale proven S2 content/social channels; stop S3 probe; small D_E09 enterprise test.
results['targeted_ads'] = nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'content_marketing': {'S2': 1500.0, 'D_E09': 100.0},
    'social_media': {'S2': 500.0},
    'linkedin': {'D_E09': 250.0},
    'search_ads': {},
    'referral_program': {},
})
# Cheap capacity headroom for scaled acquisition and higher quotas.
results['capacity'] = nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1)
# Weekly social response to rate-limit/support complaints, tied to actual quota/ops changes.
post = "We heard the workflow-limit/support feedback. Pro daily workspace is now 5k units, Starter 1k, and we doubled support coverage so workflow blockers get same-day triage. Keep telling us where it hurts."
results['social_post'] = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post)

for k,v in results.items():
    print(f"\n== {k} ==")
    print(v)

        "day": 28,
        "content": "**Question for the community: Has NovaMind's rate-limiting caught anyone else mid-workflow?**\n\nI was processing a comprehensive market analysis batch for three commercial property valuations this morning\u2014the kind of detailed comparative work that typically consumes 4-5 hours of manual research. Lost roughly two hours of processed data that hadn't been exported.\n\nBefore I escalate with support, I'm curious: Is this a known constraint at the Professional tier, or should I have structured my workflow differently? For solo practitioners managing multiple concurrent client deliverables, this workflow interruption is problematic.\n\nThe tool's capabilities are genuinely impressive\u2014the quality of its output justified my subscription investment.
